selected_important_turn.text: I can do that. Would you also be able to give me one firewood?
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn followed the opponent's explicit, transactional offer and immediately expanded the speaker's claim. That move turned the exchange into a stronger zero-sum negotiation early on and set a tone of claiming extra value rather than jointly creating value. It therefore materially affected the framing and direction of the whole interaction.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although it succeeded (the opponent later agreed), it is improvable because accepting then immediately asking for more risks appearing opportunistic and can erode trust or prompt retaliation in other contexts. It missed a chance to convert the opponent's transactional stance into a more integrative exchange (e.g., by proposing a mutually beneficial trade or offering a concession).
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Instead of immediately adding a new demand on top of acceptance, frame the follow-up as conditional or reciprocal (e.g., propose a small concession of your own in exchange, or ask if they'd be willing and note what you can give back). Alternatively, confirm the original agreement first and then, if necessary, present the extra request framed as a low-cost ask or as part of a trade-off.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: moderate_increase — reframing the extra request as part of a trade or offering a concession could uncover mutual gains and avoid zero-sum escalation.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — making the ask conditional or offering something in return signals reciprocity and fairness.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — reducing the appearance of opportunism lowers friction and the chance of stand-offs in other negotiations.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: increase — a more reciprocal framing preserves trust and rapport.

value2reason.High: It is the most important element.

value2reason.Low: I get faint if I don't eat every 2 hours and need to make sure I have enough food on hand to keep my blood sugar up
value2reason.Medium: I need to make sure my body temperature doesn't get too low, it could be life threatening
value2reason.High: I have a lot of medication to take and need to consume a lot of water

dialogue.text: Hi, looking forward to camping. Wondering if you could help me out. I really need extra water.🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I also need water.  I can give you 2 waters for 2 foods.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I can do that. Would you also be able to give me one firewood?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm okay with that offer, but I'd like to propose a small trade. If you give me one more Firewood package, I can give you one Food package. It would help balance our needs better.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I'm willing to trade one Food for one Firewood, but I need to ensure I still get at least two Water packages. Let’s see if we can find a fair balance.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm happy to trade one Food for one Firewood, but I need to make sure I still get at least two Water packages. Let’s finalize this so we can both prepare properly.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I agree to the trade. You get one Food, I get one Firewood. Let’s confirm the final split: I take 2 Water, 2 Food, 1 Firewood; you take 1 Water, 1 Food, 2 Firewood. That’s fair and covers all packages.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
